titleStmt.title: Introduction to the site of Wofoyuan

div.head: Wofoyuan
div.p: The cave temples at Wofoyuan in Anyue county 安岳, the by far largest site of Buddhist cliff carvings in Sichuan province, host sacred Buddhist texts taken from twenty-one sutras, which are distributed over the interior walls of fifteen caves that were cut out of the rock for that purpose around the Kaiyuan 開元 era (713-741) of the Tang dynasty.

The entire site of Wofoyuan encompasses 125 caves and open niches. While some of the caves are seemingly unfinished or left empty, the niches stretching along the cliffs in between the cave entrances display very fine examples of Sichuanese Tang sculptures. Most impressive is the Giant Reclining Buddha situated at the north cliff, a twenty-three meter long statue representing Śākyamuni Buddha entering final nirvāṇa, surrounded by his disciples and the eight dragon kings 八龍王. The Buddha’s final nirvāṇa thus provides the leitmotif for the entire site, and is also reflected in the selection of carved text passages, in the first place in the chapters taken from the Sutra of the Great Decease (
Mahāparinirvāṇa-sūtra
佛說大般涅槃經 ), but also in selected passages from other scriptures.
